contact_information.occupation.answer: Freelance graphic designer and part-time marketing coordinator

coaching_background.how_you_heard_and_wh.answer:
I first heard about your coaching services through a friend from my coworking space, Jenna Morales, who worked with you last year. She knew I’d been complaining (a lot) about my home office getting out of control and how it was starting to affect my business. She forwarded me one of your newsletters and a link to your website, and I ended up watching a couple of your short videos on clutter and productivity. I also saw your name mentioned in a local San Diego moms’ Facebook group where several people recommended you specifically for helping with home offices and paper clutter.

The short version is: my physical environments have gotten to a point where they’re clearly holding me back, and I feel like I’ve hit my own limit on trying to fix it by myself.

Longer version:

I’ve been working primarily from home for the last four years, doing freelance graphic design plus a part-time marketing role for a small eco-friendly skincare company. I started out pretty organized, but over time my office, dining room, and even my bedroom have turned into a mix of work materials, kids’ school papers (I have two kids, ages 8 and 11), unopened mail, half-finished craft projects, and random things that don’t have a home. I keep thinking I’ll take one weekend to “just deal with it,” but I end up moving piles from one surface to another and never really finishing.

I want a Success Strategy Session because I’m noticing specific ways this clutter and lack of systems are hurting me:

1. **Focus and productivity at work:**
   - I get distracted constantly by stacks of paper and random things in my line of sight. I’ll sit down to design a brochure, notice a pile of receipts I haven’t entered, start sorting them, then remember an email I never answered. I end days feeling busy but not actually productive.
   - I have a hard time finding client notes and sketches. There have been a couple of embarrassing moments where a client has asked a follow-up question and I’ve had to say, “Let me track that down,” because I couldn’t quickly find my original notes.

2. **Time management and procrastination:**
   - I underestimate how long tasks will take and overbook myself. I also procrastinate on things that require dealing with clutter, like filing paperwork or clearing out my email inbox, because it feels overwhelming and I don’t have a clear system.
   - I’ve paid two late fees in the last six months because bills got buried in piles of mail on the kitchen counter. That was a wake-up call that this isn’t just an aesthetic problem; it’s affecting my finances.

3. **Family stress and home environment:**
   - My husband is fairly tidy and gets frustrated with the constant “projects” spread out everywhere. We’ve had arguments where he feels like the house is never really relaxing because there’s always a mess in the background.
   - My kids have started saying things like, “I don’t know where this goes,” or “I’ll just put it on Mom’s desk,” which makes me realize I’m modeling disorganization for them.

4. **Mental load and guilt:**
   - I carry this low-level guilt all the time that I should be more on top of the house and my work systems. I’ll sit down to read or go for a walk and immediately think, “You should be sorting that box in the hallway instead.” It’s draining.
   - I’ve tried reading organizing blogs and watching YouTube videos, but I get overwhelmed by all the advice and end up doing nothing or starting something I don’t finish.

What I’m hoping to get from a Success Strategy Session with you:

- An outside, experienced perspective on what’s really going on with my clutter and time management—not just “I’m messy,” but what habits and patterns are driving this.
- Help identifying **one or two key areas** to focus on first (I suspect it’s my home office and paper management) instead of trying to overhaul everything at once and burning out.
- A realistic, step-by-step plan that fits my actual life as a working mom, not a fantasy schedule where I magically have six free hours every weekend.
- Concrete strategies for:
  - Managing incoming paper (mail, school forms, business receipts, client contracts).
  - Keeping my workspace clear enough so I can actually think.
  - Structuring my days so I’m not constantly reacting and switching tasks.
- Some accountability and encouragement. I tend to get excited at the beginning of a project, then lose momentum when things get tedious or emotional (like sorting sentimental items or old client files I feel guilty about).

Overall, I want to feel like the spaces I live and work in are supporting me instead of draining me. I’m not aiming for perfection or a Pinterest-perfect home; I just want to be able to find what I need quickly, follow through on my commitments, and feel calmer and more in control of my time and environment. I’m hoping this session will give me clarity, a starting point, and a few practical tools I can actually stick with long term.
